phoneticUs: d瑟s藞s忙t瑟sfa瑟d藢d瑟藞s忙t瑟sfa瑟d
meaning: adj.涓嶆弧鎰忕殑,涓嶉珮鍏寸殑
examples: Miller professed himself dissatisfied with Broadway theatre.
If you are dissatisfied with our service, please write to the manager.
Eighty-two percent of voters are dissatisfied with the way their country is being governed.
Is it really that women are more likely to cheat and be dissatisfied if they work?
But, he said, his men were dissatisfied with the level and quality of the training.
